title: Rāg Bāhār
text:
Āye ritu pati
āju bahata sugandha

title: Rāg Bāhār
text:
Āye ritu pati
āju bahata sugandha

title: Sangīt Manjarī
text:
The Sangīt Manjarī was first published in 1907 under the patronage of Narendra Lall Khan, the king of Nārājole and Midnapore. This site presents a careful transliteration of the original Bengali notation into Roman script, preserving the swara-marks, octave indicators, and tāl structure of the source.
Compositions are catalogued by rāga. Each page shows the lyrics followed by a notated table aligned to the relevant tāl cycle.

Transliterated by Dr. Suparno Bhattacharyya. Published by SIMA — the Society for Indian Music and Arts.

title: Rāg Nāyakī kānaḍā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

āyo phāguna baḍho māna
nanda ko chaila ḍole gokula ki ḍagara

Antarā

baḍho bhāga anurāga yā braja ko
bājata jhānjha ḍapha nanda kī bagara

Sanchārī

raṅga picakārī sabana pai ḍārata
sudha na rahe kachu aba ghara ghara

Ābhog

ānanda umangī calo braja māhi
gvāla bāla saṅga kara dhara dhara








NoteReading the notation





Dhamār is a 14-beat cycle divided as 5 + 2 + 3 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the fourteen columns correspond to beats 1–14, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 6, O (khālī) at beat 8, and 3 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Behāg
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

āju raṅga khelata hori baraja me dhuma maco ri

Antarā

śyāmasundara braja nāgara ke rasa dekhata guruyana corī

Sañcārī

gāve bajāve rasa upajāve raṅga bhara kañcana kī picakārī

Ābhog

cuvā candana aura aragajā abīra liye barajorī








NoteReading the notation





Dhamār tāl is a 14-beat cycle divided as 5 + 2 + 3 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the fourteen columns correspond to beats 1–14, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 6, O (khālī) at beat 8, and 3 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide); superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Darabāri kānaḍā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

acala birāja ho siṃhāsana baiṭhe
rājādhirāja rājārāma pṛthvī para

Antarā

takhata bakhata tumhi dino karatāra
nistāra karana ko, ānanda bhayo taha pura ghara ghara

Sanchārī

aneka aneka bhātanako naga naga takhata śobhā deta
māno indu jyota jhala mala kara

Ābhog

tānasena tua guṇa kaha na sakata
astuta karata saba loga aura tuhi jahāna para-eka nara








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Āḍānā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

aba mori pāyelā bājanu solālana tihāre darasa hama kaise āu

Antarā

jāgata hai mori nanadī jeṭhaniyāāvana ke samaya na pāu








NoteReading the notation





Tintāl is a 16-beat cycle divided as 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 9, and 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Bahār
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

āju bahata sugandha pavana sumanda madhura basanta me
hara makura para yutha madhupa madahara nirata kara rava kuñja me

Antarā

kahi koyaliyā kuhu karahi āmuvā ke ḍāra raṅga me
kahi beli cāmeli gulāba gendā campa raṅga biraṅga me

Sanchārī

ita jobana madamātī yuvati jvali rahī bina kāntame
pūkāra ghana hā nātha nātha vihata bhai prāṇāntame

Ābhog

suni śravaṇa rava raṅganātha kahata bacāve nātha kusaṅga me
ehi raṅga ḍhaṅga anaṅga mada so utārī rākha susaṅga me








NoteReading the notation





Tīvrā tāl is a 7-beat cycle divided as 3 + 2 + 2. Each row below shows two consecutive cycles side-by-side: the first seven columns form one cycle (sam X at beat 1, vibhāgs 2 and 3 marking beats 4 and 6), and the next seven columns form the following cycle.

title: Rāg Bāhār
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

āye ritu pati vasanta rāja,sakala dala sājana kara bolekoyeliyā rava sunata, śravaṅa kuhu ravamomana dara pāve

Antarā

praphullita saba vana ghanacampā bakula belari sabaati sugandha bahata pavanamanda manda ali kulavyākula bhaye








NoteReading the notation





Suraphāktāl is a 10-beat cycle. In this notation, it is divided as 4 + 2 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, and 3 at beat 7.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Suraṭ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

bāja rahī sakhiyā re muraliyā tana mana sura nāma dhāma dhara
jāgī nanadī ḍara pāi āli aba

Antarā

bakhata na jāna niṭhura muraliyā āju gayo sakhi prāṇa ,
dharama karama gayo pītama kāraṇa āju rākho mero māna mohana aba








NoteReading the notation





Tintāl is a 16-beat cycle divided as 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 9, and 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Iman kalyāṅ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

baṅgari mori murakai jina chuo laṅgaravā
kara ke kalāi ḍarana lāgī nibhara ḍara jeṭhānī deta gārī

Antarā

saṅga ke lugāi saba dūra mura musakāya gai
kara ke pharaka erī








NoteReading the notation





Ektāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Rāmkali
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

bājata bīna pravīna rabāba kinnara
gāvata indra darabāra

Antarā

ughaṭata madhura dhuna cahudisa chāyo
aura tāna mṛdaṅga dhuṅkāra

Sanchārī

siṃhāsana para baiṭhe surapati
nirakha koṭi mahajyota hāra

Ābhog

yaha sabhā ko sāja dekhe saba
mana me ānanda pāve apāra








NoteReading the notation





Jhāpatāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 3, O (khālī) at beat 6, and 3 at beat 8.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).
